2. For each client, search Gmail for the most recent email exchange (either direction). Note the date of last contact.

3. Identify any client where last contact is 60+ days ago and the relationship was not formally closed (no "goodbye" or "final invoice" type wording in the last message).

4. For each candidate, read the last 3 emails of the thread to remember context. Draft a short re-engagement message in my voice:
   - One specific reference to the work we did or discussed
   - One concrete update or offer (e.g., "I am taking on new projects starting [month]", "I built a new thing that reminded me of your use case")
   - One direct ask (e.g., "Want to catch up on a call?", "Is the X project still on your radar?")
   - Under 120 words. Conversational, not salesy.

5. Write all drafts to /home/workspace/Records/Reengagement/[YYYY-MM-DD].md. Each draft includes: client name, last contact date, the thread context summary in one line, the drafted message ready to copy.

6. Do not send anything. These are drafts for me to review and send manually.
expectedOutput: A weekly file of draft re-engagement messages for clients gone quiet, with context and ready-to-send copy. No auto-sending.
customization: Adjust the staleness threshold. Add a tone variant (more formal vs. friendly) based on client type. Filter out clients flagged as "do not contact".

name: Daily AI Tool Watch
prompt:
Give me one short daily briefing on what is moving in the AI developer tools space.

1. Search X for the last 24 hours of high-engagement posts (200+ likes) from these patterns:
   - "Claude", "Cursor", "Copilot", "Codex", "Cline", "Aider", "Zed" with release or launch or announcement context
   - "new model" or "open weights" with provider names
   - "shipped" or "launched" with AI coding tool keywords

2. Web search the last 24 hours for: "AI coding assistant release", "new LLM benchmark", "open source AI tool launch". Skip aggregator sites; favor primary sources (vendor blogs, GitHub releases, project pages).

3. Filter for things I have not seen before by comparing against /home/workspace/Records/AIWatch/seen.json (an array of {url, date}). Append new items to seen.json after processing.

4. Pick the top 3 most notable items.

5. Email me with subject "AI Watch [date]". For each item:
   - One-line summary of what shipped or changed
   - Why it matters for someone building with AI tools (1 sentence, no hype)
   - Direct link to the primary source

6. If there were no new items, send: "AI Watch [date]: quiet day, nothing new worth reporting."
expectedOutput: A 6am weekday email with three or fewer concrete things that actually shipped in AI dev tools, with primary-source links and zero hype filler.
customization: Add or remove specific tools you care about. Adjust the engagement threshold to filter signal. Restrict to weekends only if weekday volume is too noisy.

name: Trash Day Eve Reminder
prompt: It is Sunday evening, the night before trash and recycling pickup. Send the user an SMS using send_sms_to_user that says: 'Trash + recycling out tonight. Don't forget the kitchen bag.' Keep it under 140 characters. Do not include any other content.
expectedOutput: A short SMS Sunday evening reminding the user to take the trash and recycling to the curb.
customization: Change the day, the pickup items mentioned, or the delivery channel to email or Telegram.

name: The Friday Inbox Sweep
prompt: It is Friday afternoon. Use use_app_gmail with tool gmail-find-email to list unread messages from the past 7 days in the primary inbox. Group them into three buckets: (1) Needs a reply before Monday, (2) Read later this weekend, (3) Probably ignore. For each item include sender, subject, and a one sentence reason. Then send the user an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Friday Inbox Sweep' with the three buckets as markdown sections. Do not send any replies on the user's behalf.
expectedOutput: A Friday afternoon email sorting the week's unread Gmail into reply, read later, and ignore piles.
customization: Adjust the lookback window, the bucket definitions, or the day and time the sweep runs.

name: Sunday Pantry Snapshot
prompt: Read the markdown file at /home/workspace/Home/pantry.md which the user updates by hand. Parse the checklist style entries (lines beginning with - [ ] or - [x]). Build a list of items marked low or out. Send the user an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Sunday Pantry Snapshot' listing those items as a clean shopping list, plus any items that have been low for more than two weeks based on dates in the file. Keep the email under 200 words.
expectedOutput: A Sunday morning email with a tidy shopping list of low or out pantry items pulled from a local markdown file.
customization: Change the file path, the checklist format the agent parses, or the threshold for flagging long standing low items.

name: School Pickup Weather Window
prompt: Use web_search to fetch the hourly forecast for the user's city for the window 2pm to 4pm local time. Identify rain, snow, lightning, or wind above 25 mph. If any of those are present, send an SMS via send_sms_to_user that names the condition and the exact hour it lands, plus a one line suggestion like 'grab the rain jacket' or 'park closer than usual'. If the window is clear, do nothing.
expectedOutput: A conditional SMS sent only when the school pickup window has bad weather, with the exact hour and a practical suggestion.
customization: Change the pickup window hours, the city, or the weather thresholds that trigger an alert.

name: Pharmacy Refill Lead Time
prompt: Read the user's medication list at /home/workspace/Health/meds.md. Each entry has a name, daily dose, and pill count last updated date. For each medication, estimate days of supply remaining. If any medication will run out in 5 days or fewer, send a single SMS via send_sms_to_user listing those medications and how many days are left. Do not send anything if every medication has more than 5 days left.
expectedOutput: A morning SMS only when one or more prescriptions are within 5 days of running out, listing exactly which ones.
customization: Change the lead time threshold, the file path, or the delivery channel.

name: The 10pm Tomorrow Briefing
prompt: It is 10pm. Use use_app_google_calendar to list tomorrow's events on the primary calendar. Use web_search to get tomorrow's weather and any travel advisories for the user's city. Build a Telegram message with three sections: 'On the calendar' (events with times), 'Weather' (high, low, precipitation chance, notable conditions), 'Heads up' (one or two practical notes such as 'leave 15 minutes earlier, snow likely on the pass'). Send via send_telegram_message. Keep under 1000 characters.
expectedOutput: A nightly Telegram message summarizing tomorrow's calendar, weather, and any practical heads up notes.
customization: Change the time, the calendar, the city, or the three section structure.

name: Library Book Due Buzzer
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/library.md, a markdown table of borrowed books with title, due date, and renewable flag. Find any book due within 2 days. If there are any, send a single SMS via send_sms_to_user listing them with title and due date, and note which ones can still be renewed. If none, send nothing.
expectedOutput: A morning SMS only when library books are due within 2 days, including which ones can be renewed.
customization: Change the lead time, the file format, or the channel.

name: Birthday Card Mail-By Date
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/birthdays.csv (columns: name, birthday, address_known). For any birthday landing in the next 10 to 14 days where address_known is yes, treat this as the mail-by week. Send the user an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Mail a card this week' listing each name, their birthday, and the suggested mail-by date (birthday minus 3 days). Skip anyone without an address on file.
expectedOutput: A Monday email naming everyone whose birthday card needs to be in the mailbox this week.
customization: Change the lookahead window, the source file, or the buffer days for postal travel time.

name: The Hydration Nag
prompt: Send a single short SMS via send_sms_to_user telling the user to drink water. Rotate between five lines so it doesn't feel robotic: 'Water break.', 'Pour a glass.', 'Top off.', 'Drink up.', 'Refill the bottle.' Pick one based on the current hour modulo 5. Under 40 characters.
expectedOutput: A short rotating water reminder SMS three times during the workday.
customization: Change the times, the rotation of lines, or the channel.

name: Vet Visit Countdown for the Dog
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Pets/lucky.md which lists upcoming vet appointments, vaccine due dates, and flea/tick treatment schedule. Find anything due in the next 21 days. Send a Monday email via send_email_to_user titled 'Lucky vet countdown' with a short list: item, due date, days away. If nothing is in the next 21 days, send a one line note: 'Nothing on Lucky's schedule in the next three weeks.'
expectedOutput: A Monday email with the dog's next three weeks of vet related items, or a short all clear if nothing is pending.
customization: Change the lookahead window, the file path, or the pet's name.

name: The Sunday Meal Map
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/meals/this-week.md which lists planned meals for the coming week as a markdown table (day, meal, key ingredients). Also read /home/workspace/Home/pantry.md to check which key ingredients are already on hand. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Sunday Meal Map' with two sections: the week's plan as a table and a deduplicated shopping list of only the ingredients NOT already in the pantry.
expectedOutput: A Sunday email showing the week's meal plan and a shopping list filtered against what is already in the pantry.
customization: Change the file paths, the week start day, or the table format.

name: Tuesday Pollen Heads-Up
prompt: Use web_search to look up the pollen forecast for the user's city for the next 5 days. Identify the day with the highest count and the day with the lowest. Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user with the format: 'Pollen this week: peak [DAY] ([level]), easiest [DAY] ([level]).' Keep under 100 characters.
expectedOutput: A Tuesday morning SMS naming the worst and easiest pollen days of the week ahead.
customization: Change the city, the day the briefing runs, or the channel.

name: Receipts to Folder Sorter
prompt: Use use_app_gmail with gmail-find-email to find emails received today with subjects containing 'receipt', 'order confirmation', 'invoice', or 'payment'. For each match, save the email body and any PDF attachment to /home/workspace/Finance/receipts/YYYY/MM/sender-subject.pdf or .md. Create folders as needed with run_bash_command. Do not send any notification. Just sort and save.
expectedOutput: A silent daily job that files the day's receipt and invoice emails into a clean year/month folder structure.
customization: Change the subject keywords, the destination folder layout, or run it weekly instead of daily.

name: The Coupon Expiry Pinger
prompt: Use use_app_gmail to search the inbox and Promotions tab for the past 60 days for messages containing 'coupon', 'expires', '% off', or 'code'. Extract any expiration date you can find in the body. Build a list of coupons that expire within the next 14 days. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Coupons about to expire' with a table of store, code, discount, expires on. Skip duplicates.
expectedOutput: A Saturday email listing coupons from your inbox that expire in the next two weeks, with codes and discount amounts.
customization: Change the lookahead window, the search keywords, or the day of the week.

name: Town Council Meeting Watch
prompt: Use web_search to find the user's town or city council agenda for any meeting scheduled this week. If found, use read_webpage to grab the agenda text. Build a plain English summary: meeting date and time, top 5 agenda items, any item flagged for public comment. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Council meeting this week'. If no meeting is scheduled, send a one line note saying so.
expectedOutput: A Monday email with this week's council agenda summarized in plain English, or a short note that no meeting is scheduled.
customization: Change the city, the day the watch runs, or how many agenda items to summarize.

name: County Road Closure Alert
prompt: Use web_search to check the state or county DOT page for road closures and chain restrictions affecting the user's county for today. If there are any active closures or chain laws on the roads the user typically travels, send a single SMS via send_sms_to_user naming the road and the restriction. If nothing is active, send nothing.
expectedOutput: A weekday morning SMS only when there are active road closures or chain restrictions in the user's county.
customization: Change the roads watched, the county, or what counts as alert worthy.

name: The Camper Propane Check
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/camper-log.md which has weekly entries of propane tank level (in percent or pounds), date, and notes. Find the most recent entry. If the level is at or below 30 percent, send an SMS via send_sms_to_user saying so and recommending a refill before the next cold snap. If above 30 percent, send a short status SMS like 'Propane fine, [level] as of [date].'
expectedOutput: A Sunday evening SMS with current propane status pulled from a hand maintained log, plus a refill nudge if it is low.
customization: Change the threshold percent, the log file location, or the channel.

name: Phone Photo Backup Sanity Check
prompt: Use use_app_google_drive to count files in the 'Google Photos' or 'Camera Uploads' backup folder modified in the past 7 days. Compare to the prior week's count if you can find a saved counts file at /home/workspace/Home/photo-backup-counts.csv. Append this week's count to that file. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Photo backup check' with this week's count, last week's count, and a clear flag if this week is more than 50 percent below last week (possible sync issue).
expectedOutput: A Sunday night email comparing this week's photo backup count to last week, flagging suspicious drops.
customization: Change the folder watched, the drop threshold, or how many weeks of history to keep.

name: Birthday Gift Idea Stash
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/birthdays.csv and /home/workspace/Contacts/gift-ideas.md. For any birthday landing in the next 60 days, pull any gift idea notes the user has saved about that person from gift-ideas.md. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Gift ideas to start sourcing' that lists each upcoming birthday with the captured ideas. If no ideas exist for someone, suggest one open question the user could text them to gather hints.
expectedOutput: A monthly email pairing upcoming birthdays with already saved gift ideas, and a question to ask when there are none.
customization: Change the lookahead window, the source files, or the cadence.

name: Plant Watering Pulse
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/plants.md which lists each plant with its watering interval in days and the date last watered. Compute days since last watered for each plant. Build a Telegram message with three sections: 'Water today', 'Water in 1-2 days', 'Fine for now'. Send via send_telegram_message. Keep under 800 characters.
expectedOutput: A Telegram message twice a week sorting plants into water now, water soon, or fine.
customization: Change the days the pulse runs, the file format, or add a 'mist only' category for tropicals.

name: The Laundry Forecast
prompt: Use web_search to fetch the Saturday and Sunday forecast for the user's city. Pick the best day for line drying based on: sun, low humidity, low wind that is not zero, no rain. Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user with the format: 'Best laundry day: [Sat or Sun]. [reason in 6 words].'
expectedOutput: A Friday evening SMS naming the better weekend day to do laundry, with a six word reason.
customization: Change the city, the day window, or the factors that count toward 'best'.

name: Daily Step Goal Heckle
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Health/steps.csv (date, steps). Get today's steps. If today is below 5000, send a needling SMS via send_sms_to_user: pick a random heckle from ['Couch is winning today.', '5k or it didn\'t happen.', 'Lucky needs a walk anyway.', 'You owe yourself ten minutes.']. If above 5000, send a one line 'good day' style note. If no entry for today, send nothing.
expectedOutput: A 7pm SMS that either heckles the user toward more steps or briefly acknowledges a good day.
customization: Change the threshold, the heckle lines, or the time it fires.

name: Sunset Walk Window
prompt: Use web_search to look up today's sunset time for the user's city, plus the next two hours of weather. If conditions during the hour before sunset are walkable (no rain, no thunderstorm, wind under 25 mph, temp above 25F), send an SMS via send_sms_to_user with: 'Sunset walk window: [start time] to [sunset time]. [temp]F.' If unwalkable, send nothing.
expectedOutput: An afternoon SMS naming the exact hour before sunset that is good for a walk, only when conditions are clear.
customization: Change the city, the weather thresholds, or how far before sunset to start the window.

name: The Mystery Charge Detector
prompt: Use use_app_gmail to search for bank and card transaction emails from the past 7 days (filters: 'transaction', 'charged', 'receipt', 'authorized'). Extract merchant name and amount. Group by merchant. Flag any merchant the user has NOT seen in the prior 90 days of similar emails (a new payee). Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Mystery charges this week' listing only the new or unusual ones with date and amount. Skip everything familiar.
expectedOutput: A Monday email listing only charges from merchants that haven't appeared in the user's email in the past 90 days.
customization: Change the comparison window, the keywords searched, or the threshold for what counts as new.

name: The Family Group Chat Recap
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/family-notes.md where the user dumps quick lines about family conversations during the week. Summarize what each named family member shared, mentioned, or needed help with. Then suggest three small things the user could do or say in the coming week to follow up. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Family recap and 3 follow ups'.
expectedOutput: A Sunday night email summarizing the week's family touchpoints with three specific follow up ideas.
customization: Change the source file, the family members tracked, or the number of follow up suggestions.

name: Morning Stretch Trigger
prompt: Send a single SMS via send_sms_to_user with one of these five stretch prompts rotated by day of week: Mon 'Hip flexors, two minutes.', Tue 'Cat cow, ten reps.', Wed 'Shoulder rolls, both ways.', Thu 'Calf wall stretch, 30s each.', Fri 'Standing forward fold, breathe.' Keep under 60 characters.
expectedOutput: A weekday morning SMS giving a single named stretch to do before the day starts.
customization: Change the stretch prompts, the time, or the days it fires.

name: Wikipedia Article of the Day Pocket
prompt: Use read_webpage to fetch https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page and extract the 'Today's featured article' section. Write a tight 4 sentence summary in plain English, plus three follow up topics the user might want to read about next. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Wiki Pocket: [article title]'. Include the link to the full article at the bottom.
expectedOutput: A morning email with a 4 sentence summary of Wikipedia's featured article plus three related topics to explore.
customization: Change the source (e.g. Featured Picture, On This Day), the summary length, or the channel.

name: PR Stale Reviewer Nudge
prompt: Run `gh pr list --state open --json number,title,author,createdAt,reviewDecision,reviewRequests --repo $(cat /home/workspace/Projects/active-repo.txt)` via run_bash_command. Parse the JSON. Identify any PR open more than 3 days with no review decision and at least one pending review request. Build a markdown list: PR title, number, days open, reviewer handles. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Stale PRs needing review' to the user. Do not nudge reviewers directly. The user pushes them.
expectedOutput: A twice weekly email listing open PRs that have been waiting on a named reviewer for more than three days.
customization: Change the staleness threshold, the repo file path, or which repos to scan.

name: Stripe MRR Drift Alarm
prompt: Run `stripe subscriptions list --limit 100 --status active -d 'data[].items'` via run_bash_command and compute current MRR in USD by summing per period unit amounts normalized to monthly. Append today's date and MRR to /home/workspace/Finance/mrr-history.csv using create_or_rewrite_file. Compare today's MRR to yesterday's. If the absolute change is over 5 percent in either direction, send an SMS via send_sms_to_user with the prior value, today's value, percent change, and direction. Otherwise send nothing.
expectedOutput: A morning SMS only when daily MRR drifts more than 5 percent up or down, with the numbers and direction.
customization: Change the alert threshold, the currency, or the history file location.

name: The Postmortem Drafter
prompt: Check /home/workspace/Projects/incidents/inbox/ for any new file added today (a one line incident note from the user). If found, for each note: read it, then grep_search related log files under /home/workspace/Projects/logs/ for the time window mentioned. Draft a postmortem at /home/workspace/Projects/incidents/drafts/YYYY-MM-DD-slug.md using the template: Summary, Timeline, Detection, Impact, Root Cause (best guess), Open Questions, Action Items. Mark the file [DRAFT]. Do not delete the original inbox note.
expectedOutput: A dated postmortem draft file written into the incidents/drafts folder whenever a new incident note appears.
customization: Change the inbox path, the template sections, or which log files to grep.

name: Vercel Build Failure Digest
prompt: Run `vercel ls --json` and `vercel inspect <deployment> --json` via run_bash_command across the user's project list at /home/workspace/Projects/vercel-projects.txt. Collect any deployments in the past 24 hours with state ERROR or CANCELED. For each, capture project, branch, commit hash, error type, and the last 15 lines of the build log. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Vercel failures last 24h' with a section per failure. If no failures, send nothing.
expectedOutput: A morning email only when Vercel had failed or canceled deployments in the past day, with project, branch, and tail of the build log.
customization: Change the project list, the lookback window, or what counts as a noteworthy failure.

name: NPM Audit Auto-Triage
prompt: For each repo path listed in /home/workspace/Projects/repos.txt, cd into it and run `npm audit --json` via run_bash_command. Parse vulnerabilities. Group by severity (critical, high, moderate, low). For each critical and high, identify whether a fix is available without breaking changes. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'NPM audit Monday' with a per repo table: severity, package, fix available, breaking. Skip dev only dependencies unless severity is critical.
expectedOutput: A Monday email listing critical and high npm vulnerabilities across the user's repos, with which ones have non breaking fixes.
customization: Change the repos scanned, the severity threshold, or whether to include dev dependencies.

name: The Cold Email Reply Salvager
prompt: Use use_app_gmail to find cold outbound emails the user sent in the past 14 to 30 days (sent folder, no reply in thread, not in a known existing client conversation). Build a list of recipients who never replied. For each, draft a 3 sentence soft follow up that does not nag, asks a different question than the original, and offers an easy out. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Salvage these cold replies' with the list, original subject, and draft follow up text. Do not send anything to the prospects yourself.
expectedOutput: A Wednesday email with drafted soft follow ups for cold outreach that has gone silent 2 to 4 weeks ago.
customization: Change the silence window, the follow up tone, or how many to draft per week.

name: SEO Rank Slip Watch
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Marketing/tracked-queries.csv (columns: query, target_url). For each row, use web_search to find the current Google rank of target_url for that query (top 30 results only). Append today's results to /home/workspace/Marketing/rank-history.csv. Compare against the rank one week ago. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Rank movement this week' with two tables: 'Slipped 3+ positions' and 'Climbed 3+ positions'. Skip queries with no change.
expectedOutput: A Monday email showing which tracked queries climbed or slipped at least three positions in Google over the past week.
customization: Change the tracked queries, the movement threshold, or the comparison window.

name: Indie Hacker Launch Radar
prompt: Use read_webpage to fetch the Indie Hackers 'Milestones' page and the Product Hunt daily 'Top' page. Pull the top 10 of each. For each item, capture name, one line description, and link. Filter to launches that match the user's interest tags in /home/workspace/Marketing/interest-tags.txt (e.g. 'ai writing', 'developer tools', 'productivity'). Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Launch radar today' with two short sections. Cap at 6 items total.
expectedOutput: A daily email with up to six indie launches matching the user's interest tags from Indie Hackers and Product Hunt.
customization: Change the interest tags, the sources scanned, or the daily cap.

name: Linear Cycle Burn Pulse
prompt: Use use_app_linear to list issues in the currently active cycle for the team in /home/workspace/Projects/linear-team.txt. Count total issues, completed, in progress, and not started. Compute days remaining in the cycle. Estimate completion rate at current pace (completed per day so far) and forecast end of cycle. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Cycle pulse: [team] [cycle name]' with the numbers and a one paragraph honest read: on track, tight, or slipping.
expectedOutput: A mid week email with a numeric snapshot of the active Linear cycle and an honest on-track/tight/slipping verdict.
customization: Change the team, the day the pulse runs, or the forecasting math.

name: Product Hunt Competitor Sniff
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Marketing/competitor-keywords.txt (lines of keywords like 'ai writing assistant', 'content studio'). Use read_webpage on https://www.producthunt.com/ to grab today's launches. For each launch, check if any of its name, tagline, or topics contain any of the keywords (case insensitive). If matches exist, send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'PH competitors today' listing each match with link and tagline. If none, send nothing.
expectedOutput: A daily email only when a Product Hunt launch matches the user's competitor keyword list, with links and taglines.
customization: Change the keyword file, the matching logic, or whether to include the previous day's launches too.

name: The Lighthouse Score Weekly
prompt: For each URL in /home/workspace/Projects/lighthouse-urls.txt run `npx lighthouse <url> --output=json --quiet --chrome-flags='--headless'` via run_bash_command and parse the four category scores (performance, accessibility, best practices, SEO). Append a row to /home/workspace/Projects/lighthouse-history.csv with date and scores. Compare against the prior Monday. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Lighthouse Monday' with a table of current scores and the delta from last week. Flag any category that dropped 5 or more points.
expectedOutput: A Monday email with current Lighthouse scores for tracked URLs and any category that lost 5+ points week over week.
customization: Change the URL list, the drop threshold, or the audit categories tracked.

name: Postgres Slow Query Roundup
prompt: Run a query via psql in run_bash_command against the database in env var DATABASE_URL: SELECT query, calls, mean_exec_time, total_exec_time FROM pg_stat_statements ORDER BY total_exec_time DESC LIMIT 10. Format the results into a markdown table truncating query text to 120 chars. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Top 10 slow queries this week' with the table plus a one line suggestion per query (missing index, large scan, N+1 shape, etc.) where you can guess. Reset pg_stat_statements after sending only if /home/workspace/Projects/pgstat-reset.flag is present.
expectedOutput: A Monday email with the database's ten slowest queries ranked by total time, plus a guess at why each is slow.
customization: Change the ranking metric, the row cap, or whether to auto reset stats each week.

name: The Cron Job Health Audit
prompt: List all automations in /home/workspace/Projects/automations/ and any cron definitions in /home/workspace/Projects/*/crontab files. For each, capture name, schedule, last successful run timestamp (from any log file alongside it). Identify (a) automations that have not run in 30+ days, (b) automations with overlapping schedules, (c) automations with no log file at all. Send via send_email_to_user titled 'Monthly cron audit' with three sections, one per category. Suggest delete, fix, or investigate per item.
expectedOutput: A monthly email auditing every scheduled job for staleness, schedule overlap, or missing logs.
customization: Change the staleness threshold, the directories scanned, or the audit categories.

name: AWS Cost Anomaly Catcher
prompt: Run `aws ce get-cost-and-usage --time-period Start=$(date -d 'yesterday' +%F),End=$(date +%F) --granularity DAILY --metrics UnblendedCost --group-by Type=DIMENSION,Key=SERVICE` via run_bash_command. Compare yesterday's per service spend to the 7 day trailing average per service. For any service where yesterday is 2x or more the trailing average AND the dollar delta is at least 5 USD, send an SMS via send_sms_to_user listing the service, yesterday amount, and trailing average. Send nothing if no anomalies.
expectedOutput: A morning SMS only when a single AWS service spiked to at least double its 7 day average yesterday with a real dollar delta.
customization: Change the multiplier threshold, the minimum dollar delta, or which services to ignore.

name: Substack Subscriber Cohort Note
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Writing/substack-subscribers.csv exported from Substack (columns: email, signup_date, source, post_first_seen). Build cohorts by week of signup for the past 8 weeks. For each cohort report: size, top 3 sources, top 3 entry posts. Save a refreshed snapshot to /home/workspace/Writing/cohort-snapshots/YYYY-MM-DD.md. Send a short email via send_email_to_user titled 'Substack cohorts last 8 weeks' with the table and one observation about which post or source is pulling.
expectedOutput: A Sunday email with the past eight weeks of Substack signups broken down by cohort, top sources, and entry posts.
customization: Change the cohort window, the CSV path, or how many top sources and entry posts to surface.

name: Open Source Star Velocity Watch
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Projects/watched-repos.txt (lines of owner/repo). For each, run `gh api repos/{owner}/{repo} --jq '.stargazers_count'` via run_bash_command. Append today's count to /home/workspace/Projects/star-history.csv. Compute weekly delta and 4 week trailing average. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Star velocity Monday' with a table: repo, current stars, last week delta, 4 week avg delta, and a flag for any repo where this week's delta is 2x the 4 week average (heating up).
expectedOutput: A Monday email tracking weekly star gain across watched repos and flagging the ones suddenly heating up.
customization: Change the watched repo list, the heat threshold, or the comparison window.

name: The Weekly Content Atom Counter
prompt: Walk /home/workspace/Writing/ and count this week's outputs by type: posted essays (files with frontmatter status: published), drafts (status: draft), captured ideas (files in /Writing/ideas/), and atomized snippets (files in /Writing/snippets/). Compare to last week's counts saved in /home/workspace/Writing/atom-counts.csv and append this week. Send an email via send_email_to_user titled 'Content atom count' with the table and a one paragraph honest read on whether the funnel from idea to published is healthy or clogged.
expectedOutput: A Friday evening email counting this week's writing pipeline by stage with an honest read on where it is clogged.
customization: Change the directories scanned, the stage definitions, or the comparison window.

name: Dentist Six-Month Tap
prompt: Send the user an SMS that reads: 'Six-month dental tap: book your cleaning this week. Reply DONE when scheduled.' Use send_sms_to_user with no contact_name so it routes to the account owner. Keep the message under 160 characters.
expectedOutput: A short SMS twice a year prompting the user to book a dental cleaning.
customization: Change the months in the cron to match your last visit, or swap to Email if you ignore texts.

name: Library Book Due Glance
prompt: Search Gmail for messages from the last 21 days matching 'library' OR 'hold' OR 'due' OR 'overdue'. Use use_app_gmail with the gmail-search-emails tool. Pull subject, sender, and snippet for each hit. Build a single email with two sections: 'Due in the next 7 days' and 'Holds ready for pickup'. If nothing matches, send an email body that says 'No library activity this week.' Send via send_email_to_user with subject 'Library: this week'.
expectedOutput: A Sunday evening email listing upcoming due dates and ready holds from your library account emails.
customization: Edit the search terms to match your specific library system's wording, or change to daily.

name: Trash Night Reminder
prompt: Send an SMS that reads exactly: 'Trash night. Bins to the curb before bed.' Use send_sms_to_user with no contact_name. Do not add commentary or weather context. Keep it one line.
expectedOutput: A two-line SMS on collection eves so the bins go out before you forget.
customization: Change the cron days to your local pickup schedule, or add recycling on alternate weeks.

name: Family Birthday Two-Week Warning
prompt: Use use_app_google_calendar with the google-calendar-list-events tool to read events from a calendar named 'Birthdays' or 'Family' for the window starting 14 days from today and ending 15 days from today. If exactly one birthday falls in that window, send an email titled 'Birthday in 14 days: <name>' with three suggested gift ideas under $50 and a draft text message the user could send the day-of. If no birthday falls in the window, do not send anything. Use send_email_to_user.
expectedOutput: A morning email exactly 14 days before each family birthday with gift ideas and a draft message.
customization: Rename the source calendar, change the lead time, or raise the gift budget.

name: Friday Restaurant Pick
prompt: Use web_search to find one well-reviewed restaurant within 15 minutes of the user's stored location that they have not been sent in the last 8 weeks (keep a running list at /home/workspace/Notes/restaurant-history.md, read it before picking and append the new pick after). The query should target Google Maps style review aggregators. Pick a place with 4.4+ stars and at least 50 reviews. Send an SMS with the name, the cuisine, and the approximate wait time if available.
expectedOutput: A Friday afternoon SMS with one restaurant pick that has not been suggested recently.
customization: Edit the rating floor, the travel radius, or the rotation window.

name: Sunday Meal Prep List
prompt: Read the file at /home/workspace/Home/meal-rotation.md which contains a markdown table of 14 recurring meals with their ingredient lists. Pick 4 dinners and 2 lunches that have not appeared in the last 3 weeks (track at /home/workspace/Home/meal-history.md, read before selecting and append after). Build a consolidated shopping list deduplicated by ingredient with quantities summed. Email it with subject 'Sunday meal prep, week of <date>'. Include a prep order: what to roast first, what to chop, what to portion.
expectedOutput: A Sunday morning email with the week's meal picks and a deduplicated grocery list in prep order.
customization: Edit the source rotation file or change the rotation cooldown window.

name: Pollen Count Morning Alert
prompt: Use web_search for 'pollen count today <user city>' from pollen.com or weather.com. Parse the tree, grass, and weed levels. If any of the three is at 'High' or 'Very High', send an SMS that names which pollen type is high and one practical step (shut windows, take antihistamine before going out, shower before bed). If all are Low or Medium, send nothing.
expectedOutput: A morning SMS only on days when pollen is high enough to actually matter.
customization: Change the threshold to also alert on Medium, or extend to year-round.

name: Oil Change Mileage Watch
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/vehicle-log.md which contains the user's last odometer reading, date, and miles-per-month estimate. Project today's estimated mileage. If the estimated mileage is within 500 of the next 5,000-mile multiple from the last oil change reading, send an email titled 'Oil change due soon' with the estimated current mileage, the target mileage, and a one-line reminder to update the log file after the next fill-up.
expectedOutput: A monthly email only when projected mileage is near the next oil change interval.
customization: Change the interval to 7,500 or 10,000 miles for synthetic oil.

name: Tax Document Round-Up
prompt: Use use_app_gmail to search the inbox for messages from the last 45 days matching '1099' OR 'W-2' OR 'tax document' OR 'tax form' OR '1098'. For each hit, pull sender, subject, and any attachment filenames. Group by sender. Build an email body listing what has arrived and a separate section listing common tax document types that are NOT yet present in the inbox (1099-NEC, 1099-INT, 1099-DIV, W-2, 1098 mortgage interest, brokerage 1099-B). Send via send_email_to_user with subject 'Tax docs status: <date>'.
expectedOutput: Two annual emails laying out which tax forms have arrived and which are still outstanding.
customization: Add or remove document types in the search list to match your specific situation.

name: Vet Annual Shot Reminder
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Pets/vet-log.md which lists each pet, their last rabies date, last DHPP or FVRCP date, and last heartworm test date. If any vaccine or test is within 60 days of expiring (1 year from last date), send an email titled '<Pet name>: vet visit due in <N> days' with the specific vaccines or tests that need refreshing. If nothing is due, do not send.
expectedOutput: A monthly email only when a pet's vaccinations or annual tests are coming due.
customization: Add tests like dental cleaning or change the lead time from 60 days.

name: Plant Watering Wednesday
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/plants.md which lists each plant, its watering interval in days, and its last watered date. Compute which plants need water today (last watered + interval <= today). Send an SMS listing only the plants that need water, comma-separated, with a count at the end. Example: 'Water: pothos, snake plant, monstera (3 plants).' If nothing needs water, send 'Plants happy. Nothing due.'
expectedOutput: A Wednesday SMS naming exactly which plants need water this week.
customization: Edit the plant list and intervals, or add a separate fertilizing schedule.

name: Phone Storage Cleanup Nudge
prompt: Send a Telegram message that suggests three specific cleanup actions for the user's phone storage. Rotate through this list across months: month 1 ('Delete screenshots older than 30 days, clear WhatsApp media cache, review Photos shared albums'); month 2 ('Offload unused apps, clear Safari history, delete podcast downloads you have already heard'); month 3 ('Review Downloads folder, clear voicemails, archive old iMessage threads'). Track which month was last sent at /home/workspace/Home/phone-cleanup-state.md.
expectedOutput: A monthly Telegram message with three rotating, specific phone cleanup actions.
customization: Edit the rotation list to match Android or your own cleanup habits.

name: Roth IRA Contribution Window
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Finance/retirement-log.md which lists Roth contributions for the current tax year. Compute remaining contribution room against the standard limit ($7,000, or $8,000 if user is 50+, defined in the same file). Send an email with the current contribution total, remaining room, days until the April 15 deadline for the prior tax year (only relevant in Jan-April), and the dollar amount per remaining month that would max it out.
expectedOutput: A monthly email during contribution season with exact remaining room and a per-month pace number.
customization: Change the limit, swap to Traditional IRA tracking, or add 401(k) tracking.

name: Doctor Annual Physical Nudge
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Health/physicals.md which tracks the date of the last annual physical and any bloodwork. If the last physical is more than 11 months ago, send an email with the exact date of the last visit, the months elapsed, and a short script the user can paste into the patient portal to request a visit. If the most recent visit is under 11 months, do not send.
expectedOutput: A monthly email that fires only once a year, after 11 months without a physical.
customization: Change the cadence to 6 months for chronic conditions, or add specialist visits.

name: Vacation Auto-Reply Setter
prompt: Use use_app_google_calendar to list today's events with the keyword 'PTO', 'OOO', 'Vacation', or 'Travel' in the title. If found, use use_app_gmail with gmail-create-draft to draft a vacation auto-reply email addressed to a placeholder 'self@reminder' with subject 'OOO draft: paste into Gmail vacation responder'. Body should include the date range pulled from the event and a short message: 'Out of office until <end date>. For urgent items, contact <emergency contact placeholder>. I will reply when I return.' Do not actually enable the vacation responder; just stage the draft so the user can copy it in.
expectedOutput: A Gmail draft staged any morning a PTO event is on the calendar.
customization: Edit the message template or wire it to actually toggle Gmail's vacation responder via API.

name: Driver License Expiry Watcher
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/ids.md which lists the expiration dates for driver license, passport, vehicle registration, and any professional licenses. For each, compute days until expiry. If anything expires within 120 days, send an email titled 'IDs expiring soon' with each item, the date, and the renewal channel (DMV online, USPS passport, county clerk, etc). If nothing is within 120 days, do not send.
expectedOutput: A monthly email only when an ID is within four months of expiring.
customization: Add fishing licenses, CDL endorsements, or change the lead time.

name: Smoke Alarm Battery Twice-Yearly
prompt: Send an SMS that reads: 'Smoke alarm battery swap day. Test every detector and replace any 9V older than 6 months. Reply DONE when finished.' Send via send_sms_to_user, no contact_name.
expectedOutput: A twice-yearly SMS that lines up with the daylight saving change-out tradition.
customization: Move the dates, add CO detector callouts, or include a checklist link.

name: Refrigerator Leftover Audit
prompt: Send an SMS that reads: 'Fridge audit: open the door, toss anything older than Sunday, plan a leftover dinner before grocery day.' One line. No commentary.
expectedOutput: A Thursday evening SMS prompting a fridge sweep before the weekend shop.
customization: Move the day to whichever night precedes your grocery run.

name: Mortgage Payment Heads-Up
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Finance/recurring-bills.md which lists the mortgage due date, amount, payee, and account last 4. Send an email titled 'Mortgage due <date>: $<amount>' with a short body confirming the autopay account if listed, or instructions to schedule manually. Also list any other large bills due in the next 7 days from the same file.
expectedOutput: A monthly email five to seven days before the mortgage hits, with adjacent large bills.
customization: Change the bills tracked, the lead time, or swap to SMS.

name: Streaming Subscription Audit
schedule: 0 10 1 1,4,7,10 * (10am on the 1st of Jan, Apr, Jul, Oct)
prompt: Use use_app_gmail to search receipts from the last 90 days from these senders: Netflix, Hulu, Disney, Max, Peacock, Paramount, Apple, Spotify, YouTube, Amazon Prime, Audible, Patreon. Build a table of service, monthly cost, and last charge date. Total them. Send an email titled 'Streaming audit: $<total>/month across <N> services' with the table sorted by cost descending and a final line suggesting the single service watched least often based on the user's notes file at /home/workspace/Home/streaming-watch.md if it exists.
expectedOutput: A quarterly email listing every active streaming subscription with its real cost.
customization: Add or remove sender names, or change to monthly.

name: Free Trial Cancel Day Alarm
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Finance/free-trials.md which is a markdown table with columns: Service, Trial Start Date, Trial Length Days, Cancel URL. Compute days remaining for each (trial start + length - today). If any trial has exactly 2 days remaining, send an SMS naming the service and the cancel URL. If nothing matches, send nothing.
expectedOutput: An SMS exactly two days before any tracked free trial converts to a paid subscription.
customization: Change the lead time to 1 or 3 days, or switch to Email.

name: Daylight Saving Clock Sweep
schedule: 0 11 8 3 0 (11am on the 2nd Sunday of March, approximate)
prompt: Send an email titled 'Clock sweep checklist' with a list of every clock and timed device in a typical home that does not auto-adjust: oven, microwave, car dashboards, wall clocks, wristwatches, smoke alarm test buttons, sprinkler timers, thermostats without wifi, coffee maker. Add a final line: 'Test smoke alarms while you are at it.'
expectedOutput: An email twice a year listing every analog clock and timer that needs human adjustment.
customization: Edit the device list, add the November date, or convert to SMS.

name: Lawn Mowing Weather Window
prompt: Use web_search to pull a 4-day forecast for the user's city. Identify the day in the next 4 days with the lowest rain probability and a high temperature between 55 and 85 F. Send an SMS naming that day, the rain percentage, and the high. Example: 'Best mow window: Saturday, 10% rain, 72F.'
expectedOutput: A Thursday SMS picking the best lawn-mowing day from the upcoming weekend window.
customization: Adjust the temperature range, the day-of-week trigger, or the forecast horizon.

name: Family Group Catch-Up Nudge
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Personal/family-touch-log.md which lists each family member and the last date you contacted them. Pick the one person who has gone the longest without contact and is over 14 days. Send an SMS that names them and suggests one concrete opening line (a question about their recent thing, a memory, a photo to send). After sending, append today's date to that person's row.
expectedOutput: A Sunday SMS naming one family member who is overdue for contact, with an opener.
customization: Edit the family list, change the threshold from 14 days, or switch to weekly digest.

name: Anniversary Surprise Lead Time
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Personal/anniversaries.md which lists each anniversary (wedding, dating, first date, etc) with month-day. If any anniversary is exactly 21 days out, send an email with the anniversary name, the date, and three planning prompts: 'What did you do last year, what would surprise them, what would they actually want.' Include one reservation reminder and one gift idea bucket. If no anniversary is 21 days out, do not send.
expectedOutput: An email exactly 21 days before each anniversary with planning prompts.
customization: Change the lead time, add more anniversaries, or add a 7-day follow-up.

name: Weekly Town Council Agenda Pull
prompt: Use web_search to find the user's town or city council agenda for the upcoming week. The query should be '<user city> town council agenda <current month> <current year>'. Use read_webpage on the most authoritative result (city .gov domain preferred). Extract: meeting date, time, location, agenda item titles. Send an email with a clean bulleted list of agenda items and any item that mentions zoning, taxes, budget, or police, flagged in bold.
expectedOutput: A Monday email summarizing the upcoming town council meeting agenda with flagged hot topics.
customization: Change the city, the flagged keywords, or the day of the week.

name: Local Election Two-Week Warning
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Civic/elections.md which lists upcoming election dates for the user's state and county. If any election is exactly 14 days out, use web_search to find the official sample ballot for that election in the user's county (search '<county> <state> sample ballot <election date>'). Send an email titled 'Vote in 14 days: <election name>' with the polling location lookup link for the user's state and a bulleted list of ballot items pulled from the sample ballot. If no election matches, do not send.
expectedOutput: An email exactly two weeks out from any tracked local or federal election, with the ballot preview.
customization: Edit the elections file, change the lead time, or add early voting deadlines.

name: Stripe MRR Daily Snapshot
prompt: Run a bash command that calls the Stripe API (curl with the STRIPE_SECRET_KEY env var) to fetch active subscriptions (/v1/subscriptions?status=active&limit=100, paginate). Sum the monthly value across all active subs, normalizing yearly to monthly by dividing by 12. Save today's MRR to /home/workspace/Finance/mrr-history.csv as a new row (date, mrr_usd, active_subs). Compute delta vs yesterday and delta vs 30 days ago from the CSV. Send an email titled 'MRR: $<value> (<+/->$<delta> day, <+/->$<delta> 30d)' with a 7-day sparkline drawn in ASCII characters.
expectedOutput: A daily email with current MRR, day-over-day delta, 30-day delta, and a 7-day ASCII trend line.
customization: Add ARR, customer count, churn count, or split by product.

name: Vercel Build Failure Watch
prompt: Run a bash command that calls the Vercel API (curl with VERCEL_TOKEN env var) at /v6/deployments?limit=10. Parse the JSON and check the readyState of the most recent deployment per project. If any project's most recent deployment has state ERROR or CANCELED, and you have not already alerted on this deployment id (track at /home/workspace/Ops/vercel-alert-state.json), send an SMS naming the project and the deployment URL. Save the deployment id after alerting so you do not double-fire.
expectedOutput: An SMS the moment any tracked Vercel project's latest deploy fails, fired once per deployment.
customization: Filter to specific project names, change the polling interval, or route to Discord.

name: Cloudflare DNS Record Drift
prompt: Run a bash script that calls the Cloudflare API (curl with CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN env var) listing DNS records for every zone the token can read. Snapshot the full record set to /home/workspace/Ops/dns-snapshot.json. Diff against the previous snapshot at /home/workspace/Ops/dns-snapshot.prev.json. If any record was added, removed, or changed (type, content, proxied), send an email titled 'DNS drift detected in <zone>' with the diff in a readable format. Rotate the current snapshot to .prev after the diff.
expectedOutput: An email only when DNS records change across any zone you manage.
customization: Add an allowlist of records expected to change, or scope to one zone.

name: NPM Audit Vulnerability Sweep
prompt: For each repo path listed in /home/workspace/Dev/repos.txt, run 'npm audit --json' (or 'pnpm audit --json' if pnpm-lock.yaml present) and capture output. Parse total vulnerability counts by severity. Build a report at /home/workspace/Dev/audit-reports/<YYYY-MM-DD>.md listing each repo with critical/high/moderate/low counts and the top 5 advisories with their fix availability. Email a summary listing only repos with any critical or high vulnerabilities, ordered by critical count desc.
expectedOutput: A Monday email and dated markdown file showing every repo with critical or high npm vulnerabilities.
customization: Add pip-audit for Python repos, raise the severity threshold, or auto-open PRs for fixes.

name: SaaS Trial-to-Paid Drop-Off Watch
prompt: Query the user's Postgres or SQLite app database via psql or sqlite3 (connection string in DATABASE_URL env var) for users whose trial ended in the last 24 hours. For each, fetch their signup date, their last login, the number of core actions taken during trial (defined in /home/workspace/Dev/trial-events.sql), and whether they converted to paid. Build a table grouped by 'Converted' and 'Did Not Convert', sorted by core-action count desc within each group. Email with subject 'Trial cohort: <date>, <X> converted of <Y>'.
expectedOutput: A daily email showing yesterday's expiring trial cohort, who converted, and how active each was.
customization: Change the conversion windows, the event definitions, or add per-source attribution.

name: ProductHunt Top 5 Recap
prompt: Use read_webpage on https://www.producthunt.com/ to grab today's top 5 products by upvote count. For each, extract name, tagline, upvote count, and category tag. Send a Telegram message with each product on its own block, formatted like: '1. <name> (<votes> upvotes) - <tagline>'. Include the ProductHunt URL of the page at the bottom.
expectedOutput: An evening Telegram digest of today's top 5 ProductHunt launches.
customization: Change to top 10, filter by category tag, or switch to Discord.

name: Backlog Aging Heat Map
prompt: Use use_app_linear to list all open issues in the user's primary team across all projects. For each issue, compute days since createdAt. Bucket into: 0-7 days, 8-21 days, 22-60 days, 61-180 days, 180+ days. Build an HTML email with one bar per bucket, the bar width proportional to issue count, and a list of the 5 oldest issues with title, days old, and assignee. Subject: 'Backlog aging: <N> issues, oldest <days>d'.
expectedOutput: A Monday morning email with a visual age distribution of your Linear backlog plus the 5 oldest items.
customization: Filter by project, label, or priority, and change the age buckets.

name: Cold Outreach Reply Detector
prompt: Use use_app_gmail to search for replies in the last 2 hours to a specific outreach thread label, configured at /home/workspace/Sales/outreach-label.txt (default: 'Outreach/Sent'). For each new reply, classify the response into one of: Positive (interested, asks a question), Neutral (acknowledgment), Negative (not interested, unsubscribe), Auto-reply. Send an SMS only for Positive replies, with the sender name, company if extractable from the signature, and the first 80 characters of the reply.
expectedOutput: An SMS within 2 hours of any positive reply landing on a tracked outreach thread, weekdays only.
customization: Change the label, the polling cadence, or add Negative replies for damage control.

name: Postgres Slow Query Hunter
prompt: Run psql (DATABASE_URL env var) to query pg_stat_statements for the top 10 queries by total_exec_time over the last 7 days where calls > 50. For each, capture: normalized query, total time, mean time, calls, rows. Build an email with the table sorted by total_exec_time desc, plus a short note flagging any query with mean_exec_time > 500ms. Reset stats only if explicitly told via /home/workspace/Ops/reset-stats.flag existing.
expectedOutput: A Monday email naming the 10 most expensive Postgres queries from the past week.
customization: Change the window, add per-database breakdowns, or route to Discord.

name: Discord Community Pulse
prompt: For each channel id listed in /home/workspace/Community/discord-channels.json, use get_discord_history to pull the last 7 days of messages (limit 1000). Compute: total message count, unique active members, top 5 emojis used, top 5 most-replied-to messages. Build an email with one section per channel showing those numbers. At the bottom, list any new members whose first message appeared in the last 7 days. Subject: 'Discord pulse: <total> messages across <N> channels'.
expectedOutput: A Monday email summarizing last week's Discord activity per channel with new-member callouts.
customization: Change the channels, the window, or add sentiment analysis of top threads.

name: Founder Inbox VIP Tier Sort
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Inbox/vip-tiers.json which has three tiers: Tier 1 (investors, top customers), Tier 2 (warm leads, advisors), Tier 3 (everyone else). Use use_app_gmail to list unread emails from the last 24 hours. Match each sender to a tier. Build an email with three clearly separated sections, Tier 1 first, listing sender, subject, and one-line auto-generated reason it likely matters. Drop Tier 3 entirely unless it is empty above. Subject: 'Inbox: <T1> Tier 1, <T2> Tier 2, <T3> Tier 3'.
expectedOutput: A twice-daily email organizing unread mail by your hand-built VIP tiers, with Tier 3 hidden.
customization: Edit the tier list, change the polling cadence, or add a Tier 0 for spouse and family.

name: Weekly Competitor Pricing Page Diff
prompt: Read the list of competitor pricing page URLs at /home/workspace/Competitors/pricing-urls.txt (one URL per line). For each URL, use read_webpage to fetch the page as markdown. Save the new content to /home/workspace/Competitors/snapshots/<domain>-<YYYY-MM-DD>.md. Diff against the most recent prior snapshot for that domain. If any pricing-relevant string changed (any line containing '$', '/mo', '/year', 'free', or 'plan'), include the before/after lines in the email body. Group by domain. If no diffs anywhere, send an email body 'No pricing changes detected this week.'
expectedOutput: A Tuesday email showing every competitor pricing page change since last week, with diffs.
customization: Add or remove competitor URLs, change the diff keywords, or move to daily.

name: GitHub Star Velocity Tracker
prompt: For each repo listed in /home/workspace/Dev/watched-repos.txt (format: owner/repo, one per line), run 'gh api repos/<owner>/<repo>' and capture stargazers_count. Append today's count to /home/workspace/Dev/star-history.csv (repo, date, stars). Compute 1-day, 7-day, and 30-day star deltas from the CSV. Build an email sorted by 7-day delta desc, table format. Flag any repo with a 1-day delta > 3 standard deviations above its 30-day mean as 'spiking'.
expectedOutput: A daily email ranking watched repos by 7-day star velocity with spike callouts.
customization: Add forks and watchers, change the spike threshold, or scope to your own repos.

name: Reddit Brand Mention Sweep
prompt: Read the list of brand terms at /home/workspace/Brand/mention-terms.txt (product name, founder handle, domain, common misspellings). For each term, use web_search with query 'site:reddit.com "<term>"' restricted to the last 4 hours via the time_range parameter. For each new hit not already in /home/workspace/Brand/seen-urls.txt, send a Telegram message with the subreddit, the post title, the URL, and the first 200 chars. Append the URL to the seen file after sending.
expectedOutput: A Telegram ping within 4 hours of any new Reddit mention of your tracked brand terms.
customization: Add Hacker News and X mentions, change the cadence, or filter by subreddit.

name: Self-Improving Prompt Library Audit
prompt: List every file in /home/workspace/Prompts/ recursively. For each .md prompt file, read it and check three things: does it have a 'last_used' date in frontmatter, has it been used in the last 60 days (cross-reference /home/workspace/Prompts/usage-log.csv), and does the prompt contain any token from /home/workspace/Prompts/forbidden-tokens.txt (one word per line, user-maintained slop blocklist). Build a report at /home/workspace/Prompts/audit-<YYYY-MM>.md listing: prompts unused 60+ days, prompts that hit the blocklist, prompts missing frontmatter. Email a summary count of each bucket with the file paths.
expectedOutput: A monthly email and dated audit file flagging stale, sloppy, or under-documented prompts.
customization: Edit the forbidden-tokens file, change the staleness window, or add token-length flags.

name: Launch Week Engagement Decay Chart
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Marketing/active-launches.json which lists launch posts with: post_id, post_url, launch_date, end_date. Only run if today is within any active launch window. For each active launch post, use x_search to fetch current impressions, likes, reposts, replies. Append today's snapshot to /home/workspace/Marketing/launch-metrics.csv. Build an email with one section per launch showing day-by-day numbers and an ASCII chart of impressions per day. Flag the first day the daily delta drops below 25% of peak as 'decay onset'.
expectedOutput: A daily email during any active launch window tracking the decay curve of each launch post.
customization: Add cross-posts on LinkedIn and Threads, or change the decay threshold.

name: Hacker News Show Pull
prompt: Use read_webpage on https://news.ycombinator.com/show to fetch today's Show HN front page. Extract the top 10 by points: title, points, comment count, and submitter. For each, also fetch the submission URL (the link out of HN, not the comments page). Build an email titled 'Show HN today: top 10' with each item as a block (title as link to comments, points, comment count, one-line snippet from the linked site if accessible). Skip items with fewer than 10 points.
expectedOutput: An evening email with the top 10 Show HN projects of the day, scored and linked.
customization: Change to Ask HN or the main front page, raise the points floor, or move to Telegram.

name: The 5pm Dinner Decider
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/pantry.md and /home/workspace/Home/dietary-notes.md. Pick three dinner options that use mostly what is already in the pantry and fit the dietary notes. For each option, list the missing ingredients (max two per dish) and a rough cook time. If everything in the pantry is depleted, run a web_search for a single 20 minute dinner recipe with five or fewer ingredients. Send a single SMS via send_sms_to_user with the three options as a short numbered list, no preamble.
expectedOutput: A short text at 5pm with three dinner picks based on what is actually in the pantry, plus a short shopping nudge if needed.
customization: Edit pantry.md and dietary-notes.md, or change the number of options and cook time threshold in the prompt.

name: Saturday Morning Cleanup Captain
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/cleanup-rotation.md, which contains a list of weekly chores grouped by room. Pick the next three rooms in rotation that have not been touched in the longest time according to the last_done dates. Build a short Saturday checklist: each chore as a one line action with an estimated minute count. Update cleanup-rotation.md with today as last_done for the chosen rooms. Email the checklist via send_email_to_user with subject 'Saturday cleanup, 3 rooms' and the list as a markdown checklist.
expectedOutput: A Saturday morning email with three rooms to clean and a short timed checklist that rotates fairly through the house.
customization: Edit cleanup-rotation.md to add or remove rooms and chores, or change how many rooms get picked each week.

name: Birthday Card Mail-By Reminder
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/birthdays.md, which lists friends and family with name, birthday, and shipping zone. For each entry, compute days until the next birthday. If exactly 10 days remain, that person needs a card sent by today to arrive in time. Build one email listing only those people, each with name, date, and a one line gift idea drawn from the notes column in the file. If nobody is at 10 days, send no email. Use send_email_to_user with subject 'Birthday cards to mail this week' only when there is at least one match.
expectedOutput: A morning email only on days when someone needs a card mailed today to arrive on time.
customization: Change the 10 day lead window, swap shipping zones, or add a notes column with gift ideas in birthdays.md.

name: Where Did I Put That Log
prompt: Open /home/workspace/Home/where-is-it.md. This file is appended to throughout the day via voice notes or quick edits with lines like 'passport: top drawer of camper desk'. Deduplicate entries, keeping only the most recent location for each item. Sort alphabetically by item name. Write the cleaned version back to the same file with a header showing today's date as 'Last cleaned: YYYY-MM-DD'. Do not send any message.
expectedOutput: A clean, deduped index of where household items live, updated quietly every night.
customization: Add categories like 'tools' or 'documents' as sections in the file, or change the cleanup time.

name: The Did I Text Mom Check
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/keep-in-touch.md. Each row lists a person, their preferred channel, and last_contacted date. Find anyone marked 'weekly cadence' who has not been contacted in 7 or more days. If the list is empty, send a one line SMS: 'All weekly people are current.' Otherwise send an SMS via send_sms_to_user listing each overdue person on its own line with their preferred channel and how many days it has been. Keep the whole SMS under 320 characters.
expectedOutput: A Sunday evening text showing exactly which weekly-cadence people are overdue for a check-in.
customization: Add or remove cadence tiers like 'monthly' or 'quarterly' and adjust the day threshold in the prompt.

name: Weekend Hobby Spark
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/hobbies.md, which lists hobbies the user actually enjoys with rough skill level. Pick one at random, weighted toward hobbies not touched in the last month based on last_done dates. Run a web_search for one beginner-friendly project or activity for that hobby that can be done in 2 to 3 hours. Email a short note with the suggested project, the rough time, and any materials needed. Use send_email_to_user with subject 'Saturday hobby spark: <hobby name>'.
expectedOutput: A Saturday morning email with one concrete weekend project drawn from your real hobby list.
customization: Edit hobbies.md with skill levels and last_done dates, or change the time-budget bounds for suggestions.

name: Mood and Weather Pattern Log
prompt: Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user asking: 'Mood today 1 to 5? Reply with a number and one word.' When the user replies, web_search today's local weather summary for Salmon, Idaho (high temp, low temp, conditions, barometric pressure). Append a new row to /home/workspace/Health/mood-weather.csv with columns date, mood, word, high, low, conditions, pressure. If the file does not exist, create it with that header row.
expectedOutput: A nightly check-in text that quietly builds a long-term CSV of mood against local weather.
customization: Change the mood scale, ask different free-text questions, or switch the location used for the weather lookup.

name: Friday Fridge Cleanout
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/fridge.md, where each line is an item with a purchase or open date. Find items older than 5 days for fresh produce, 7 days for opened sauces, and any explicitly tagged 'leftover' older than 3 days. Build a short SMS listing the top five items most at risk of going to waste, ordered by age, and one suggested combo dinner that uses two of them. Send via send_sms_to_user. If nothing is at risk, send 'Fridge is fine.'
expectedOutput: A Friday afternoon text with the five oldest fridge items and a quick combo idea before the weekend.
customization: Edit the age thresholds per category and add new tags like 'meal-prep' or 'dairy' in fridge.md.

name: The Anti-Affirmation Scrambler
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Personal/voice-lines.md, which contains 30 to 50 short lines written by the user in their own voice (dry, honest, occasionally funny, not motivational). Pick one at random that has not been used in the last 14 days according to /home/workspace/Personal/voice-lines-used.csv. Append today's date and the line to that CSV. Send the line as a single SMS via send_sms_to_user with no quotes and no preamble.
expectedOutput: A 7am text with one honest one-liner from your own writing, rotated so nothing repeats inside two weeks.
customization: Edit voice-lines.md to add or remove lines, or change the no-repeat window from 14 days.

name: Doctor Visit Prep Sheet
prompt: Use use_app_google_calendar to list events on tomorrow's primary calendar. If any event title contains 'doctor', 'dentist', 'clinic', 'neurologist', or 'appointment', build a one page prep sheet. Pull recent entries from /home/workspace/Health/symptom-log.md (last 14 days), current med list from /home/workspace/Health/meds.md, and a short questions section from /home/workspace/Health/questions.md. Email the sheet with subject 'Prep for tomorrow: <event title>' via send_email_to_user. If no matching event exists, do nothing.
expectedOutput: An evening email the night before any medical appointment with symptoms, meds, and questions on one page.
customization: Edit the trigger keywords or the files that feed the prep sheet, and adjust the symptom lookback window.

name: The Lazy Sunday Movie Picker
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/watchlist.md, which has movies and shows the user has been meaning to watch with genre and length tags. Filter to anything under 110 minutes and tagged 'comfort' or 'low effort'. If the list has fewer than three items, web_search for one current well-reviewed feel-good movie under two hours. Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user with three picks, one per line, including title and runtime.
expectedOutput: A Sunday evening text with three low-effort, short-runtime movie picks already aligned with your taste.
customization: Edit watchlist.md, change the runtime limit, or adjust which tags count as 'comfort' viewing.

name: Library Book Due Date Watcher
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/library-books.md. Each entry has title, branch, and due_date. Compute days until due for each book. If any book is due in 2 days or less and not yet returned, send a short email via send_email_to_user with subject 'Library books due soon' listing each book, branch, and exact due date. If nothing qualifies, send nothing.
expectedOutput: A quiet morning email only when library books are within two days of being late.
customization: Adjust the warning window, add a renewal link column, or split alerts by branch.

name: Camper Battery Voltage Logger
prompt: Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user asking: 'Camper bank voltage? Reply with a number like 12.6'. When the user replies with a number, append a row to /home/workspace/Camper/battery-log.csv with columns timestamp, voltage, hour_slot. If the file does not exist, create it with that header. If the reply is below 12.2 in winter months (Nov to Mar) or below 12.0 otherwise, send a follow-up SMS: 'Low bank, consider charging today.'
expectedOutput: Two daily voltage check-ins that build a CSV log and warn when the camper bank is sagging.
customization: Change the warning thresholds per season or add additional fields like solar input and amp draw.

name: The Pantry Inventory Refresh
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/pantry.md and /home/workspace/Home/pantry-purchases.csv. Cross reference purchase dates with shelf life ranges in /home/workspace/Home/shelf-life.md. Flag any pantry item past its expected shelf life and any staple (rice, beans, flour, oats, oil, salt, pasta sauce) missing from pantry.md. Email a single Sunday note via send_email_to_user with two sections, 'Use or toss' and 'Restock'. Update pantry.md last_reviewed date.
expectedOutput: A Sunday morning email pairing pantry items that should be used up with staples that need restocking.
customization: Edit shelf-life.md, add or remove items from the staples list, or change which day the review runs.

name: Family Group Text Opener
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/family-context.md (a short page of low-stakes safe topics and shared interests). Pick one topic from the list. Optionally run a web_search for one current, friendly, non-political news item on that topic. Draft a single warm one or two sentence opener the user could paste into the family group text. Send it via send_sms_to_user as just the draft, no labels.
expectedOutput: A Wednesday evening text with one ready-to-paste opener for the family group chat.
customization: Edit family-context.md to control safe topics, or change the schedule to a different evening.

name: Dog Walk Route Randomizer
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Pets/walk-routes.md, which lists named walking routes near Salmon with rough length, terrain, and shade notes. Run a web_search for today's local weather in Salmon, Idaho. If high temp is above 80F, pick a shaded short route; if below 30F or precipitation is likely, pick a short low-elevation route; otherwise pick something the dog has not done in the last 5 days. Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user with the route name, expected length, and a one line reason.
expectedOutput: A morning text picking the right named walking route for the weather and rotation.
customization: Edit walk-routes.md to add routes or tune the temperature thresholds and rotation window.

name: Monthly Phone Photo Backup Nudge
prompt: Use use_app_google_drive to list files in the 'Phone Backups' folder. Find the most recent upload date. If the newest upload is older than 35 days, send an email via send_email_to_user with subject 'Phone photos not backed up in over a month' explaining the gap and including a checklist of three concrete steps (plug in phone, open backup app, verify folder size). If the last backup is fresh, send a short 'Photo backups look current' confirmation.
expectedOutput: A monthly email that either nudges you to back up phone photos or confirms it is up to date.
customization: Change the Drive folder name, the gap threshold in days, or the checklist contents.

name: The Lazy What I Ate Log
prompt: Send an SMS via send_sms_to_user asking 'What did you eat in the last few hours? One line, lazy is fine.' Wait for a reply. Append the timestamp and reply text to /home/workspace/Health/food-log.csv with columns timestamp, slot, text. The 1pm prompt uses slot=midday and the 8pm prompt uses slot=evening. Do not analyze, do not score, do not lecture.
expectedOutput: Two short daily texts that build a no-judgment running log of what you actually ate.
customization: Change the wording, the number of pings per day, or the slot labels in the CSV.

name: Plant Watering Days Counter
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/plants.md, which lists each plant with location and watering interval (days). Read /home/workspace/Home/plant-log.csv to find last_watered per plant. For any plant where today is greater than last_watered + interval, build a single SMS line per plant in the form 'snake plant (camper window) is due'. Send one combined SMS via send_sms_to_user only if at least one plant is due. After sending, do not auto-update last_watered; the user does that manually when they actually water.
expectedOutput: A morning text only on days when at least one plant is actually due to be watered.
customization: Edit plants.md to add plants and intervals, or split alerts by indoor vs outdoor.

name: Friday Wind-Down Playlist
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Personal/music-taste.md, which lists genres the user actually likes and a few anchor artists. Build a 12 track wind-down playlist concept that fits a 'slow Friday evening' mood. Use web_search if helpful to confirm artist names and recent albums, but do not invent tracks; if uncertain, prefer well known catalog songs. Email the list via send_email_to_user with subject 'Friday wind-down, 12 tracks' as a markdown numbered list of 'Artist - Title'.
expectedOutput: A Friday evening email with a 12 track wind-down playlist that fits your actual taste.
customization: Edit music-taste.md, change the track count, or shift the mood (focus, drive, deep work).

name: Tax Receipt Photo Catcher
prompt: Use use_app_google_drive to list files in the 'Receipts inbox' folder added since the last run (track last_run in /home/workspace/Finance/receipts-state.json). For each new file, extract the filename and upload date. Append rows to /home/workspace/Finance/receipts.csv with columns date_added, filename, drive_link, category (blank). Update last_run. Email a short Sunday summary via send_email_to_user with the count of new receipts and the first five filenames for spot checking.
expectedOutput: A weekly email confirming how many receipts were filed and prompting you to tag categories.
customization: Change the Drive folder, add automatic category guessing, or switch to a monthly cadence.

name: Car Maintenance Mileage Nudge
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Home/vehicle.md, which has current odometer (manually updated), last_oil_change_miles, oil_interval, last_tire_rotation_miles, rotation_interval, and registration_due date. If current odometer minus last_oil_change_miles exceeds oil_interval minus 300, flag oil change. Same for tire rotation. If registration is due within 30 days, flag registration. If any flags, send email via send_email_to_user with subject 'Camper truck maintenance due soon' listing flagged items. Otherwise send a one line SMS via send_sms_to_user: 'Truck is current.'
expectedOutput: A weekly check that emails when service is approaching and texts a clean bill when it is not.
customization: Update vehicle.md with new intervals, add brake pad mileage, or change the lead-time buffers.

name: Friend Birthday Card Drafter
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Contacts/friends.md, which lists name, birthday, vibe (dry, sincere, goofy), and a short notes column. Find anyone whose birthday is exactly 3 days away. For each match, draft one short handwritten-card message (3 to 5 sentences) in their preferred vibe, using one specific detail from the notes column. If nobody matches, do nothing. Otherwise email all drafts together via send_email_to_user with subject 'Card drafts to write today'.
expectedOutput: An email three days before a friend's birthday with a card draft tuned to that specific person.
customization: Adjust the lead time, the vibe categories, or the message length range.

name: Hourly Move Your Body Ping
prompt: Read /home/workspace/Health/micro-movements.md, which lists 20 micro-movements (each 30 to 90 seconds, no equipment, low impact). Pick one not used in the last 4 hours according to /home/workspace/Health/micro-movements-log.csv. Append the pick to the log. Send a single SMS via send_sms_to_user with just the movement name and the duration, no preamble. Keep tone matter of fact, not chirpy.
expectedOutput: Short workday texts with one tiny movement each hour, rotating without repeats.
customization: Edit micro-movements.md, change the active hours, or skip the prompt entirely on focus-block days.

name: Saturday Farmers Market Forecast
prompt: Run a web_search for 'Salmon Idaho farmers market today' and for the local weather forecast for Salmon, Idaho on Saturday morning. Read /home/workspace/Home/market-staples.md for the user's usual buys (eggs, greens, bread, etc) by season.
